THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE CONFIRMED A LOW END EF0 TORNADO IN CASS COUNTY INDIANA. THE TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN 2 MILES NNE OF ADAMSBORO. THE TORNADO HAD AN APPROXIMATE PATH WIDTH OF 25 YARDS AND LENGTH OF 50 YARDS. A BARN WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED WITH DEBRIS BLOWN AND LIFTED ONE EIGHTH OF A MILE TO THE NORTH AND NORTHWEST. MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS WERE DETERMINED TO BE 65 TO 70 MPH.
TV ANTENNA BY HOUSE BENT DOWN. ROOF OFF GARAGE.
LARGE TREE DOWN ACROSS ROADWAY AT 300 N JUST WEST OF US31. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
TREE DOWN AT 400 S AND 300 E. ANOTHER TREE DOWN ON SR35 JUST NORTH OF SR10...TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE CONFIRMED A LOW END EF0 TORNADO IN CASS COUNTY INDIANA. THIS TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN 1 MILE EAST OF TWELVE MILE. THE TORNADO HAD AN APPROXIMATE PATH WIDTH OF 25 YARDS AND LENGTH OF 50 YARDS. A TV ANTENNA NEXT TO A HOUSE WAS COMPLETELY BENT TO THE GROUND. A SMALL OUTBUILDNG WAS SHIFTED OFF ITS FOUNDATION AND THE ROOF WAS REMOVED AND CARRIED INTO A FIELD. MAXIMUM WINDS SPEEDS WERE DETERMINED TO BE 65 TO 70 MPH.
BARN DOWN AT 150 S AND 500 E. POWER LINES AND TREES DOWN ACROSS THE COUNTY.

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE HAS CONFIRMED AN EF1 TORNADO IN GRANT COUNTY INDIANA. THIS TORNADO BEGAN APPROXIMATELY 3.5 MILES SW OF FAIRMOUNT AND ENDED APPROXIMATELY 2.3 MILES WNW OF FAIRMOUNT. THE TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 2.5 MILES AND MAX WIDTH OF 150 YARDS. MULTIPLE HOUSES WERE DAMAGED...SIDING STRIPPED...WINDOWS BROKEN...ROOF DAMAGE INCLUDING A ROOF COMPLETELY REMOVED FROM THE BACKSIDE OF ONE RESIDENCE. NUMEROUS TREES WERE BROKEN AND SNAPPED. WINDS WERE DETERMINED TO BE 105 TO 110 MPH.

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE HAS CONFIRMED A LOW END EF1 TORNADO IN GRANT COUNTY INDIANA. THIS TORNADO BEGAN APPROXIMATELY 3 MILES SW OF UPLAND AND ENDED JUST WNW OF UPLAND. THIS TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 2.5 MILES AND A WIDTH OF 50 YARDS. A COUPLE OF HOMES SUSTAINED DAMAGE ALONG WITH HARDWOOD TREES BEING SNAPPED. THE WINDS WERE DETERMINED TO BE 90 TO 95 MPH.
32 FOOT TRAVEL TRAILER ROLLED TWICE AND DESTROYED. ROOF OF MILKHOUSE BLOWN OFF AND CONCRETE BRICK WALL BLOWN DOWN. INSULATION AND SKIRTING FROM MOBILE HOME BLOWN ABOUT. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

IN THE TOWN OF GREENWOOD...SHED FLATTENED AND BARN ROOF DESTROYED WITH ADDITIONAL DAMAGE TO PATIO AWNING. ROOF OFF A MACHINERY BUILDING. ANOTHER SHED WAS BLOWN ONTO A PICKUP. MINOR TREE DAMAGE
LARGE TREE ON TRAILER
ROOFS OFF OUTBUILDINGS. CHICKEN COOP BLOW OVER. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR. DEBRIS IN ROAD.

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE HAS CONFIRMED AN EF1 TORANDO IN JAY COUNTY. THE TORNADO SKIPPED ALONG A PATH OF ALMOST 7 MILES WITH A MAX WIDTH OF 50 YARDS. THE TORNADO BEGAN NORTH OF ANTIVILLE AND ENDED APPROXIMATELY 1.5 MILES SW OF JAY CITY. UTILITY POLES WERE SNAPPED WITH POWER FLASHES OBSERVED...NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED...A GARAGE WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED ALONG WITH A SMALL BARN AND POLE BUILDING. A COUPLE OF HOUSES ALSO SUSTAINED EF1 DAMAGE. THE WINDS WERE DETERMINED TO BE 90 TO 95 MPH.
3 POWER POLES SNAPPED. US 27 CLOSED BETWEEN 400 N AND 500 N. POWER FLASHES WERE REPORTED.
HEADER BLOWN OVER HOUSE. MULTIPLE PINE TREES SNAPPED. WINDOWS BLOWN OUT OF RESIDENCE AND GARAGE DOOR BLOWN IN. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
HEADER BLOWN OVER HOUSE. MULTIPLE PINE TREES SNAPPED. WINDOWS BLOWN OUT OF RESIDENCE AND GARAGE DOOR BLOWN IN. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE WITH THE ASSISTANCE OF THE VAN WERT COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER HAS CONFIRMED AN EFO TORNADO. THIS TORNADO HAD AN APPROXIMATE PATH LENGTH OF 0.5 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 25 YARDS. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 80 TO 85 MPH. THE TORNADO BEGAN AT THE INTERSECTION OF LIBERTY ROAD AND TOWNSHIP HIGHWAY 140 AND CONTINUED NORTHEAST CROSSING THE INTERSECTION OF SR 224 AND LINCOLN HIGHWAY BEFORE DISSIPATING. ONE HOME SUFFERED EXTENSIVE STRUCTURAL DAMAGE AND NUMEROUS PINE TREES WE
BARN DESTROYED WITH DEBRIS ACROSS CONVERSE ROAD

AN EF1 TORNADO OCCURRED IN VAN WERT COUNTY OHIO AND CONTINUED INTO ALLEN COUNTY OHIO. THIS TORNADO HAD AN APPROXIMATE PATH LENGTH OF 4 MILES AND MAXIMUM WIDTH OF ONE HALF MILE. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE IN THE 100 TO 105 MPH RANGE. THIS TORNADO BEGAN SOUTH OF MONTICELLO NEAR THE VAN WERT AUGLAIZE COUNTY BORDER AND ENDED TO THE NW OF SPENCERVILLE IN ALLEN COUNTY OHIO. DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED TO MULTIPLE STRUCTURES INCLUDING SHEDS AND POLE BARNS THAT WERE DESTROYED AND TWO HOUSES. DEBRIS WAS CARRIED
24 FOOT LONG TRAVEL TRAILER DESTOYED...3 LARGE TREES DOWN...POWER LINES DOWN...ROOF DAMAGE...GARAGE LEVELED

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE HAS CONFIRMED AN EF1 TORNADO IN ALLEN COUNTY OHIO. THE TORNADO BEGAN APPROXIMATELY 2 MILES N OF CAIRO. THE TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF ONE HALF MILE AND MAX WIDTH OF 100 YDS. SUBSTANTIAL DAMAGE TO A HOMESTEAD WAS OBSERVED INCLUDING THE LOSS OF THE ROOF AND COMPLETE DESTRUCTION OF A BARN. LARGE PINE TREES WERE SNAPPED AT THE BASES. DEBRIS WAS FOUND SCATTERED IN THE FIELD UP TO A HALF MILE AWAY. A TRAILER AND OTHER LARGE TREES WERE TOPPED AT A LOCATION ACROSS THE STRE
BARN DESTROYED...TREES UPROOTED...ROOF TORN OFF HOUSE
